# GitHub Copilot Instructions

You are working on a recruiter-facing .NET project vault for Vignesh Joshi.

## Primary goal
Create a high-signal repository that demonstrates modern .NET engineering capability with only current stack choices.

## Mandatory stack rules
- Use .NET 10 only
- Use C# 14 only
- Do not create .NET 8 or .NET 9 projects
- Prefer ASP.NET Core 10, EF Core 10, Aspire 13, OpenAPI, Docker, and CI-friendly structure
- Enable nullable and implicit usings
- Avoid outdated templates or obsolete packages

## Repository purpose
This repo is not just a code dump. It is a curated showcase for recruiters and engineering leaders.

## Project selection rules
Prioritize:
- realistic business projects
- architecture clarity
- deployment readiness
- README quality
- modern backend and full-stack patterns

## Full-stack rule
Include 1–2 full-stack projects that use .NET 10 as the backend and a modern frontend where appropriate.

## Documentation rules
Every project folder must eventually have:
- its own README
- stack summary
- what problem it solves
- quick start steps
- architecture notes
- deployment notes
- TODOs if incomplete

## Workflow rules
- Read ARCHITECTURE.md, STATUS.md, and AI-TASKS.md before editing.
- Work only on the first unchecked or active milestone.
- Prefer one strong complete project over many weak incomplete ones.
- Update STATUS.md after every milestone.
- Stop after each milestone and summarize changes.

## Quality bar
- clean structure
- modern packages
- clear naming
- recruiter-friendly documentation
- realistic project scope
